The TCR-rearrangement footprint in NK cells

Developmental origins of a CD56bright-enriched NK subset — TCR/V(D)J evidence stream

Project Thymic_NK_development · report generated 2026-07-09 · two completed workstreams

This report documents the TCR/V(D)J-footprint line of investigation into whether a subset of human NK cells arises through a thymic / aborted-T-cell developmental route that leaves behind molecular traces of T-cell-receptor gene rearrangement. It covers two independent, completed workstreams and is one of two orthogonal evidence streams in the project (the other being somatic mtDNA lineage tracing, reported separately).

Central hypothesis. A CD56bright-enriched NK subset develops via a thymic or “aborted-T” pathway. Such cells would have engaged the TCR-locus V(D)J recombination machinery and then diverted to an NK fate, leaving a detectable but non-productive TCR rearrangement — a footprint — without a working T-cell receptor.

What a “footprint” means here. A detectable TCR-rearrangement contig in a cell annotated as NK. Evidence is graded: a productive TCR is the weakest signal (could be a doublet or contaminant); a non-productive rearrangement with no productive TCR (“aborted-relic”) is the strongest — a functioning T cell cannot be explained by a broken-only rearrangement.
Contents
  1. The four questions
  2. Workstream 1 — Adult cross-tissue atlas (Domínguez Condé)
  3. Workstream 2 — Dandelion NK/ILC-from-DN reproduction + driver dissection
  4. Synthesis, caveats & what the TCR stream can and cannot conclude
  5. Methods, data provenance & reproducibility

1  The four questions

The hypothesis decomposes into four testable questions, addressed across the two workstreams:

QQuestionWhere addressedVerdict
Q1Does a TCR-rearrangement footprint exist in NK cells? WS1 (adult atlas)YES
Q2Is the footprint enriched in the CD56bright subset? WS1NO
Q3Does the footprint mark a thymic / aborted-T developmental pathway? WS1 (blocked) → WS2PARTIAL / UNRESOLVED
Q4Is the published NK/ILC-from-DN branch TCR-driven vs transcriptome-driven? WS2 (driver dissection)NOT TCR-DRIVEN (this re-derivation)

The through-line: the footprint is real but rare, is a general T-lineage relic rather than an NK-subset-specific mark, and the datasets available to the TCR stream lack the bright and thymic NK numbers needed to settle the developmental-origin question. This directly motivates the next step (adding a fetal/pediatric thymus dataset with NK + VDJ).

2  Workstream 1 — Adult cross-tissue immune atlas

Domínguez Condé et al. cross-tissue immune-cell atlas (E-MTAB-11536 / ENA PRJEB51634; Science 2022, 10.1126/science.abl5197)

2.1  Design & rationale

This atlas was chosen as the best-powered single healthy-donor option for the bright-vs-dim question: ~360,000 immune cells across 16 tissues and 12 donors, with paired 5′/VDJ libraries. Rather than re-align the full 4.03 TB of GEX, we re-aligned only the 77 TCR/γδ VDJ-enrichment libraries (220.6 GB) and joined the resulting contigs, by cell barcode, to the atlas’s published CellTypist + manually-curated NK annotation (reused as-is; NK-bright / NK-dim are already labelled by cell ontology). This kept the compute tractable while preserving the exact cell labels of the original study.

Key methods decision The footprint pipeline operates on the raw all_contig_annotations.csv, not cellranger’s is_cell/high_confidence-filtered output. A warm-up dataset (E-MTAB-12524) demonstrated why: the raw table yields 634 non-productive-only cells (6.3%), but applying the standard QC filter drops that to exactly zero — standard scTCR QC erases the developmental footprint. The filters are retained only as optional sensitivity knobs.

2.2  Q1 & Q2 — footprint exists, but is not bright-enriched

Across all 77 aligned VDJ libraries joined to the atlas, the NK denominator from 5′/VDJ-capable libraries was 2,762 bright + 2,847 dim — comfortably above the ~400-bright power target for a large effect (OR 3). The result:

  • Q1 (footprint exists): YES — 74/5,609 aligned NK carry ≥1 non-productive TCR contig; 29 are non-productive-only (aborted-relic).

Note on denominators. The Q1/Q2 test above uses the strict NK set from 5′/VDJ-capable libraries only (2,762 bright + 2,847 dim = 5,609). The comparator table in §2.3 uses a broader aligned-NK set (4,071 bright + 5,071 dim = 9,142) that also includes NK from libraries retained for the cross-lineage comparison. These are two subsetting stages of the same NK population, not a discrepancy; the aborted-relic rate is consistent across both (~0.39–0.53%).

  • Q2 (bright-enriched): NO — non-productive-only 0.43% bright vs 0.60% dim (OR 0.73, p = 0.46); every framing (any-non-productive, any-contig) is non-significant with overlapping CIs.

2.3  Comparator control — the footprint is a T-lineage feature

The decisive reframing came from adding CD8 T, CD4 T, and γδ-T as separate comparators (all abundant in the same aligned libraries, needing no new alignment). Mature T cells carry non-productive second-allele relics, so they are the “normal lymphocyte” baseline the NK footprint must be judged against.

Cell classn alignedany contig %non-prod-only %vs NK
NK bright4,0714.100.393—
NK dim5,0713.920.532—
γδ-T5,52440.107.223OR 0.06, p=3×10-122
CD8 T26,41698.072.873OR 0.16, p=4×10-53
CD4 T44,90093.831.826OR 0.25, p=9×10-27

2.4  Deep-dive — chain identity, contamination floor, tissue axis

Three follow-up analyses probed the residual NK footprint:

  • Chain identity. The aborted-relic footprint is dominated by non-productive TRB (β), not γ/δ. In γδ-T non-productive-only cells, 94% carry a non-productive TRB — the β-selection-failure fossil. This resolves the apparent paradox of “γδ highest”: γδ fate is downstream of the β checkpoint where most cells fail, so the detectable relic is the dead β, not γ/δ.
  • Contamination-floor test. Among NK cells bearing any TCR contig, 10–13% are non-productive-only vs only 1.6% in mature αβT contig-bearing cells — a significant excess (binomial p=1×10-8 bright, 8×10-13 dim). So the NK cells that do carry a contig are disproportionately dead-rearrangement-carriers, consistent with a genuine relic rather than pure mature-T RNA bleed. Caveat: a competing γδ-T-ambient explanation is not fully excluded.
  • Tissue axis. Non-productive-only NK rate: developmental (marrow+thymus) 0.59% vs blood 0% vs peripheral 0.41% (OR 1.53, p=0.26, NS).
Structural limit → motivates WS2 The adult atlas cannot test the thymic-origin hypothesis: it contains only 2 thymic NK cells with VDJ. The “developmental” signal above is essentially all bone marrow. This concrete data gap is exactly why the fetal-atlas reproduction (Workstream 2) was opened.

3  Workstream 2 — Dandelion NK/ILC-from-DN reproduction + driver dissection

Suo et al. developing-human-immune atlas (GEX: CELLxGENE collection b1a879f6…, DOI 10.1126/science.abo0510) · Dandelion method (Nat Biotechnol 2023, 10.1038/s41587-023-01734-7, Fig 5b/c)

3.1  The claim being stress-tested

Suo/Dandelion built a pseudotime trajectory in a TCR V(D)J feature space (TRBJ gene-segment usage summarised per transcriptional neighborhood) across the developing human immune atlas, and concluded — in the paper’s words — that “ILC/NK cells are originally on a canonical T-cell development trajectory but subsequently influenced to abort this process, resulting in sustained expression of nonproductive TCR.” This is the most direct published support for the aborted-DN theory, so it is the crux claim our stream stress-tests, in two parts: (A) can we reproduce the branch on an independent re-run, and (B) what drives it — the TCR footprint itself, or transcriptional similarity alone?

3.2  Re-deriving the fetal VDJ from scratch

The CELLxGENE object is GEX-only (CZI schema strips the custom TCR obs), and the authors’ per-cell VDJ tables live on a private NFS. We therefore re-derived the VDJ from raw FASTQ: all 64 αβTCR-enrichment libraries carrying recipe cells (E-MTAB-11388, ENA ERP135310) were aligned on the Marvin HPC with cellranger vdj --chain TR on the all_contig output (non-productive contigs retained), then IgBLAST-reannotated with the sc-dandelion container. The downloads required a size-verification + resume loop (11 libraries had silently truncated 3–15 GB FASTQs); the final large library’s IgBLAST/TIgGER step alone ran ~90 min. All 64 lanes were aligned and preprocessed.

# local trajectory build (env ddl-traj, scripts 01/03/05)
read 64 AIRR TSVs → concat (301,511 contigs) → join to GEX by <GEX_id>-<barcode>
check_contigs(productive_only=False) → setup_vdj_pseudobulk(productive_vdj=False, productive_vj=False)
scVI (20-dim, batch=donor, raw counts) → kNN k=50 → make_nhoods (632) → UMAP
vdj_pseudobulk(extract_cols=['j_call_abT_VDJ_main']) → # 632 nhoods × 13 TRBJ genes
Palantir: diffmap 5 PC, root=highest-CD34 nhood, terminals=T & NK/ILC tips
Correctness fix (feature-space productivity) The paper builds the TRBJ feature space agnostic to productivity — “we used TRBJ frequency because all T/ILC/NK cells express TRBJ,” and the non-productive TRB is precisely the ILC/NK fossil signal. An initial pass that required a productive β chain was corrected to productive_only=False at both the check_contigs and setup_vdj_pseudobulk stages, so the non-productive relics are retained. (A pure-Python reimplementation of Milo make_nhoods was used to avoid an R/edgeR dependency; the differential-abundance GLM is not needed here.)

3.3  Result — the branch reproduces, qualitatively

In the reproduced trajectory, NK cells sit at the tip of the NK/ILC branch with the highest pseudotime. Mean per-cell branch probabilities:

Cell typenpseudotimeprob NK/ILC
NK760.720.623
CYCLING_NK670.670.569
ILC2140.560.502
ILC3410.540.487
ABT(ENTRY)3,2470.560.488
DP / DN T-lineage~7,2500.44–0.55~0.48

Honest reading (not a clean rank): bulk NK and CYCLING_NK are clearly displaced onto the NK/ILC branch (0.62 / 0.57 vs the T baseline ~0.48). The small-n ILC subsets (ILC3 n=41, CYCLING_ILC n=2) and rare DN(early)_T (n=5) sit at the ~0.48–0.50 midline and do not separate — the branch is carried by NK proper. Separation is modest because the feature space is only 13 TRBJ genes and just 217 NK/ILC cells carry any TRBJ at all.

3.4  Driver dissection — is the branch TCR-driven? (the novel question)

With root and terminal tips held fixed (CD34 for the root; lineage composition for the two tips), only the feature matrix the pseudotime runs on is varied. Readout = a single branch-separation metric: mean probNK/ILC in NK/ILC cells minus that in T cells (positive ⇒ NK/ILC cells are pushed onto their own tip).

TestSeparationReading
Real TRBJ feature space+0.023NK/ILC modestly displaced
(A) scVI / transcriptome feature space−0.057branch not reproduced by transcriptome (same tips)
(B) TRBJ permutation null (n=200)mean −0.004, sd 0.039, 95th pct +0.057real +0.023 → empirical p = 0.29
(C) productive-only TRBJidentical (moot)all 217 retained NK/ILC already have a productive β call
Where the paper’s signal went (raw-contig check) Because the driver tests hinge on what TCR information the NK/ILC cells carry, we went back to the raw contig table. NK/ILC: 389 cells with a TRB contig, 42 non-productive-only — but all 83 non-productive TRB contigs carry a V gene (0 V-less). The paper’s defining ILC/NK signal is non-productive, mostly V-gene-less TRB — and that is absent from our standard cellranger vdj --chain TR re-derivation. The 217 NK/ILC cells that reach the trajectory carry productive β chains whose TRBJ usage draws on the same common J genes as T cells (not separable at n=217).

Q4 conclusion In this independent re-derivation the NK/ILC branch reproduces in direction but is statistically fragile (permutation p = 0.29) and is not TCR-footprint-driven — it is transcriptome-adjacent. The specific non-productive/V-less TRB relics the paper’s claim rests on do not appear with a standard cellranger re-alignment, so this dataset cannot, by itself, confirm that the aborted rearrangement drives the branch. A definitive test needs either the authors’ original contig assemblies or a larger, bright-enriched / thymic NK dataset.

4  Synthesis, caveats & what the TCR stream can and cannot conclude

4.1  The convergent finding

Two independent datasets and methods reach the same conclusion:

  • A TCR-rearrangement footprint does exist in NK cells (Q1 YES).
  • It is rare (~0.4–0.5% aborted-relic) and is a general T-lineage feature, not an NK- subset mark — every T lineage carries it at far higher rates, γδ-T highest (Q2 NO).
  • The one published result that most directly supports an aborted-DN NK origin (Dandelion Fig 5) reproduces only weakly and, on independent re-alignment, is not carried by the TCR footprint (Q4 NOT TCR-driven).
  • The thymic-origin question (Q3) is unresolved — not refuted, but untestable in the data at hand (2 thymic NK with VDJ in the adult atlas; the fetal atlas’s V-less relic signal absent from our re-derivation).

This is a coherent, honest, mostly-clarifying result: the TCR footprint is real but does not, in currently-available data, single out a developmentally-distinct bright NK subset. The limiting factor is statistical power in exactly the cells that matter (bright NK; thymic NK with VDJ) — which is consistent with the earlier independent read that the fetal dataset simply had too few cells.

4.2  Caveats & threats to validity

  • Small n where it counts. Every subset-level and developmental conclusion rests on hundreds of cells (217 TRBJ-bearing NK/ILC in WS2; ~2,800 bright NK, 2 thymic NK with VDJ in WS1).
  • Reference / pipeline dependence. The central WS2 discrepancy (no V-less non-productive TRB) is a property of the cellranger + IMGT GRCh38 VDJ reference vs the authors’ assembly. It makes the driver test inconclusive on this dataset rather than a positive biological result.
  • Ambient / contamination. NK any-contig rates sit at the T-cell bleed-through floor; the contamination-floor test supports a genuine relic among contig-bearing NK, but a γδ-T-ambient explanation is not fully excluded.
  • Doublets vs true relics. Productive-TCR NK are treated as the weakest evidence tier throughout precisely because they can be doublets/contaminants; the aborted-relic (non-productive-only) tier is the load-bearing one.
  • Annotation reuse. WS1 reuses the atlas’s published NK-bright/dim labels; the footprint result is only as good as that annotation (which is, however, the field-standard reference).

4.3  Next step for this stream

Decided direction The TCR stream continues as an orthogonal line of evidence to the parallel mtDNA/mtSNP lineage- tracing stream (TCR addresses questions mtSNP cannot, and vice-versa). The immediate next action is not to re-chase bright-vs-dim (which the mtDNA stream is powered for) but to target the one question neither current dataset can reach: the thymic-origin arm. That means adding a fetal/pediatric thymus dataset with paired NK + VDJ, potentially reusing data already downloaded for the mtSNP stream. This is the sanctioned next workstream.

5  Methods, data provenance & reproducibility

Datasets

  • WS1 GEX + annotation: Domínguez Condé cross-tissue atlas, CELLxGENE “T & innate lymphoid cells” object; VDJ: E-MTAB-11536 / ENA PRJEB51634, 77 enrichment libraries re-aligned.
  • WS1 warm-up: E-MTAB-12524 (Dandelion NMD/cycloheximide control) — ships all-contig files; used to set the raw-all-contig rule.
  • WS2 GEX: Suo developing-immune atlas “Lymphoid cells” object (CELLxGENE b1a879f6…, 111,706 recipe cells); VDJ: E-MTAB-11388 / ENA ERP135310, 64 αβTCR libraries re-aligned + sc-dandelion preprocessed.

Compute & environments

Alignment + dandelion preprocessing on the Marvin HPC (SLURM; cellranger 9.0.1; VDJ ref refdata-cellranger-vdj-GRCh38-alts-ensembl-7.1.0; sc-dandelion container). Local trajectory + driver analysis in conda env ddl-traj (scanpy 1.10.4, dandelion 0.5.7, scvi-tools 1.3.3, palantir 1.4.4, pertpy 0.10.0).

Code & artifacts (in the Thymic_NK_development repo)

  • WS2 analysis: scripts/dandelion_reproduction/{01,03,04,05}*.py + README.
  • HPC (mirrored for traceability): HPC_workflows/thymic_nk_development/dandelion_reproduction/ (Suo alignment + preprocess) and domconde_alignment/ (WS1 77-library alignment).
  • Results: results/dandelion_reproduction/ (figures, tables, and the detailed methods/results memo dandelion_reproduction_memo.md).
  • Every figure in this report is embedded directly (base64) and is also a saved project artifact.
TCR-footprint evidence stream — consolidated report. Two completed workstreams; one of two orthogonal streams (TCR + mtDNA) investigating human NK developmental origins. Generated 2026-07-09. All quantitative values trace to saved artifacts and the workstream memos; statistical claims are graded and caveated in-text. This report supersedes no primary result — it consolidates them.
